name: Camino de Santiago — circular by Rabé de la Calzada
name_original: Camino de Santiago — circular por Rabé de la Calzada
region_intro: Jogging around Tardajos offers access to established paths and natural landscapes in the province of Burgos, Spain. The area is characterized by its position on the Camino de Santiago (French Way), providing open "meseta" terrain suitable for running. Situated at the confluence of the Urbel and Arlanzón rivers, Tardajos features riverbanks and expansive meadows. These natural settings, combined with historical routes, create diverse running opportunities. Best jogging routes around Tardajos The most popular jogging route is Camino de Santiago — circular by Rabé de la Calzada, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) difficult trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route follows a section of the historic Camino de Santiago, offering a blend of cultural significance and open terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Río Arlanzón – Juventud loop from Laboratorio de autonomía avanzada, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.1 km) path. This route explores the riverbanks of the Arlanzón, providing scenic views of the surrounding meadows. Local runners also love the Paseo de la Isla loop from Laboratorio de autonomía avanzada, a 3.6 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through river walks and open spaces, often completed in about 36 minutes. Jogging around Tardajos is defined by the Camino de Santiago, riverbanks, and expansive meadows. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Tardajos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 2400 runners have used komoot to explore Tardajos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Tardajos?
A: There are over 100 running routes around Tardajos, offering a wide variety of options for all skill levels, from easy strolls to more challenging runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails near Tardajos?
A: Running routes around Tardajos primarily feature open 'meseta' terrain, characteristic of the Camino de Santiago, as well as scenic paths along the Urbel and Arlanzón riverbanks and through expansive meadows. You'll find a mix of established paths and natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Tardajos?
A: Yes, Tardajos offers several easy running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31779267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de la Isla loop from Laboratorio de autonomía avanzada&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.6-mile (5.9 km) trail that leads through river walks and open spaces, perfect for a relaxed run.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Tardajos?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051971060/camino-de-santiago-circular-by-rabe-de-la-calzada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago — circular by Rabé de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.4-mile (15.1 km) difficult trail following a section of the historic Camino.

Q: What are some natural features or landmarks I might see while running in Tardajos?
A: While running, you'll encounter the natural beauty of the Urbel and Arlanzón riverbanks and expansive meadows. The area is also rich in history, being on the Camino de Santiago. You might spot historical elements like the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Tardajos, or explore nearby attractions such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/171220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781649" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arch of Santa María&lt;/a&gt;, though these are a bit further afield.

Q: Are the running trails in Tardajos suitable for families?
A: Yes, the diverse network of routes includes options suitable for families. The flatter, open 'meseta' terrain and river walks provide accessible paths for various ages and fitness levels. Look for routes marked as 'easy' or 'moderate' for the most family-friendly experiences.

Q: Is it possible to run with dogs on the trails near Tardajos?
A: Many of the natural paths and open 'meseta' trails around Tardajos are suitable for running with dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or on sections of the Camino de Santiago where you might encounter other pilgrims and livestock.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Tardajos?
A: The running routes in Tardajos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the historical significance of the Camino de Santiago sections, and the scenic river views.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Arlanzón river?
A: Yes, you can enjoy scenic runs along the Arlanzón river. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24323997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanzón – Juventud loop from Laboratorio de autonomía avanzada&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.2-mile (13.1 km) path that offers beautiful views of the riverbanks and surrounding meadows.

Q: What is the best season for running in Tardajos?
A: Tardajos offers good running conditions throughout much of the year. The open 'meseta' terrain can be exposed in summer, so early mornings or late afternoons are ideal. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them excellent seasons for exploring the trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options available?
A: For those looking for longer runs, Tardajos is situated on the Camino de Santiago, which provides extensive paths. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38144493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Rabé de la Calzada – Tardajos loop from Los Llanos&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 22-mile (35.4 km) route that offers a substantial distance for endurance training.
tips: Small town that is located in the west of the province of Burgos and that shows a small nucleus of this flat area of the territory. It is said that Rabé comes from the name "rabbi", from the Christian religion, although it cannot be confirmed one hundred percent.
Before reaching the center of Hornillos del Camino there is a descent from where you can see the town itself and the area around it.
In this part of the Camino de Santiago we find a set of cereal fields that extend on both sides of the path.
